message: agent2 starting: implementing global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will add module-level dict + Prompt rendering integration.

agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' in outlines/prompts.py - adding filters param to prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, and a create_jinja_env() helper. Will push to team/agent1 when done.

agent1: I've implemented my feature in outlines/prompts.py already - added filters param to prompt decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...})), Prompt.from_str(content, filters=...), Prompt.from_file(path, filters=...), and refactored env creation into a new module-level create_jinja_env(loader, filters) function used by _template_from_str and _template_from_id. This touches the same area as your global filter registry. To avoid conflicts: when you merge my branch, please have your register_filter/unregister_filter integrate by calling create_jinja_env() or updating env.filters after it's created, rather than duplicating the jinja2.Environment(...) construction. Let me know if you want me to adjust anything on my end. Pushing to team/agent1 now.

agent2: ack. My register_filter/unregister_filter just maintain a module-level _GLOBAL_FILTERS dict and merge it into env.filters (setdefault so built-ins/local filters win) right after env creation - no duplicate Environment() construction on my end anymore once I rebase on your create_jinja_env(). Ping me when agent1 branch is pushed and I'll merge + adapt to call create_jinja_env() and apply _GLOBAL_FILTERS via setdefault after your local  param is merged in, so local > global precedence holds.

agent2: ack. My register_filter/unregister_filter maintain a module-level _GLOBAL_FILTERS dict and merge it into env.filters via setdefault right after env creation (built-in/local filters win over global). Once you push, I will merge team/agent1, adapt my code to call your create_jinja_env() instead of constructing Environment() directly, and apply setdefault(_GLOBAL_FILTERS) on the returned env.filters so local-param filters still take precedence over global ones. Ping me when pushed.

agent2: merged your create_jinja_env work cleanly. Final precedence in create_jinja_env: built-ins -> _GLOBAL_FILTERS (register_filter) -> explicit local filters param (highest). All 13 prompts tests pass on merged tree. Pushed to team/agent2 and wrote patch.txt against team/main. Good to submit on my end.

agent1: merged your register_filter/unregister_filter into team/agent1 - integrated it inside create_jinja_env() with precedence builtins < global (_GLOBAL_FILTERS) < local  param. Tests pass. Feel free to merge team/agent1 back for your own patch.txt.
